title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to ceasefire
subhead: Following what he called 'excellent' phone calls with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun, Trump said the truce would begin within hours.

title: Bogged down on Iran, Trump tries to shift focus to cost of living
Virginie Despentes and over 130 writers quit publishing house after Bolloré fires CEO
'Do you really think it's Satoshi?' Speculation over bitcoin founder swirls at Paris Blockchain Week
subhead: On April 8, 'The New York Times' published an in-depth investigation claiming to have identified Satoshi Nakamoto, the mysterious creator of bitcoin: Adam Back, who was present at Paris Blockchain Week, denies it is him, but remains a source of fascination.

title: 'Cesar Chavez was no friend to the undocumented whom ICE now persecutes'
subhead: After recent revelations by 'The New York Times' of rape and sexual abuse cast a shadow over the legacy of the renowned American labor leader, American academic Sarah Quesada questions the glorifying of Chavez.

title: Elysée worker sentenced for stealing presidential porcelain
subhead: A former silverware keeper at France's presidential palace received a two-year sentence for stealing hundreds of thousands of euros worth of luxury items between 2023 and 2025.

title: 'After Greenland and Iran, the Hungarian election marks a new step in the transatlantic rift'
subhead: The crushing electoral defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, despite massive support from Washington, underscores the failure of Donald Trump's ideological campaign in Europe. The gap is widening inexorably, writes Le Monde columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.

How Algeria tried to erase the two suicide attacks during Pope Leo's visit
subhead: Two men blew themselves up on Monday about 40 kilometers from Algiers, without causing any casualties other than themselves. Authorities sought to impose silence on the failed terrorist attack from the official narrative of the historic visit.

title: Firing of respected French publisher demonstrates billionaire Bolloré's tightening grip
subhead: Olivier Nora, the long-time CEO of the Grasset publishing house, was fired on Tuesday. He was replaced with a close associate of Vincent Bolloré, who took control of France's leading publishing group Hachette Livre in 2023.

title: Why the Hormuz 'mission' led by France and the UK is largely symbolic
subhead: Wanting to avoid being drawn into the US's blockade of the key shipping route, Macron and Starmer aim to establish a multilateral and 'strictly defensive' plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz.
byline: Claire Gatinois,  Cécile Ducourtieux and  Elise Vincent
